key_points: Power sharing prevents concentration of power and protects minority rights
Belgium adopted a complex federal structure to manage ethnic diversity
Majoritarianism in Sri Lanka led to discrimination against Tamil minorities
Power sharing occurs among government organs, social groups, political parties and different levels of government
Democratic stability depends on participation of diverse communities
examples: Belgium introduced equal representation of linguistic groups in central government
Community governments handle cultural and language issues
Tamil political organizations demanded an independent state called Tamil Eelam
Pressure groups influence public policies in democratic systems
formulas: Horizontal Power Sharing = Legislature + Executive + Judiciary
Vertical Power Sharing = Central Government + State Government + Local Government
Social Power Sharing = Representation of minority groups
Political Power Sharing = Coalition governments and multiparty systems
exam_tips: Compare Belgium and Sri Lanka case studies in answers
Explain four forms of power sharing with examples
Use keywords like federalism, coalition government, pressure groups
